prevalence.trend: stable with seasonal peaks; risk increases with extreme rainfall and flooding

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion (fever, myalgia, jaundice, renal failure) plus lab confirmation (MAT serology, PCR where available); Ministry of Health case definitions

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; outbreak/seasonal surveillance and syndromic detection
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Good with early doxycycline/penicillin; severe disease outcomes depend on timely hospitalization

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SUS relies on clinical suspicion with confirmatory testing in reference labs; differential includes viral hepatitis and dengue
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Early antibiotics (doxycycline, amoxicillin, or IV penicillin/ceftriaxone for severe cases) plus supportive renal/hepatic care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited confirmatory testing in some regions, and high exposure risk during floods in large cities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National notification system (SINAN) and established outbreak response protocols linked to environmental health actions
